title: Almanac resource catalog entry (v2)
description: One curated entry describing a public resource — a dataset, paper, textbook, or document — its canonical home, how to access it, whether it is still reachable, and where the original can be recovered. The catalog maps resources; it does not host them. v2 generalizes v1 from datasets to any monitored resource and adds the monitor/recovery model documented in SCHEMA-V2.md. Non-breaking sibling of catalog-entry.schema.json (v1); cutover + migration are follow-ups.

id.description: Stable kebab-case slug. Must match the filename (minus .yaml). Never reused.

type.description: Resource kind. The discriminator that lets one schema hold a dataset and a paper identically; verticals become tags, not separate machines.

description.description: One or two sentences: what the resource is and why it matters.

publisher.description: Authoritative producer — the agency, institution, or venue that publishes the resource. Reference-piece only; carries NO weight in recovery ranking.

collection.description: Optional id of a derived collection grouping entries that share a verifiable trait (a domain, a dark-date). A collection is a view, never a monitored entity; its health is computed from members, never stored.

source.description: The reference piece: where the resource canonically lives. Authority lives here and ONLY here.

canonical_url.description: Current authoritative home of the resource.

identifiers.description: Type-agnostic identifier map (replaces v1 source.doi). Known schemes typed; other schemes permitted.

method.description: How the resource is actually retrieved.

auth_note.description: What credential is needed, e.g. 'free API token'.

format.description: File/serialization formats offered, e.g. [csv, json, netcdf, pdf].

fingerprint.description: Monitor baseline captured WHILE the resource was live. Absent/null for entries cataloged after the resource died — that is honest, not a defect (honest tiers, no fiat). What makes monitoring catch silent revision a 200-check misses.

sha256.description: Hash of the canonical artifact as last seen live.

captured.description: ISO date the baseline was captured.

observed.description: Pure machine facts from the last probe. No interpretation — the machine records facts here and never assigns a lifecycle label.

checked.description: ISO date the resource was last probed (replaces v1 last_checked).

reachable.description: Did the probe get any response?

http_status.description: HTTP status of the final response.

final_url.description: Where the probe actually landed. Differs from source.canonical_url on redirect — the highest-signal monitoring fact.

fingerprint_result.description: Comparison of the probed artifact against fingerprint.sha256.

status.description: Derived lifecycle label; auditable against `observed`; human-overridable. live=present & current; revised=same URL, content drifted; moved=relocated, content verified equivalent (needs baseline); redirected=lands elsewhere, equivalence unverified (honest default); superseded=URL serves different content, original gone-in-place; dark=unreachable/404; frozen=reachable but publisher-declared static.

status_since.description: ISO date the CURRENT status was first observed — the catalog's analog of Willow's invalid_at. With `status`, the (state, boundary) pair: a record is time-bounded, never silently deleted.

status_source.description: Who assigned the current status. Curator overrides are themselves a visible fact.

recovery.description: Ranked list of where the ORIGINAL resource can be retrieved. Ranked by verifiable authenticity, NEVER by who runs the source. Authority (the publisher) appears nowhere here.

via.description: Recovery channel, e.g. wayback, cloud_mirror, repository, community, predecessor, self.

authenticity.description: Verifiable fidelity tier. hash-verified=matches our baseline; cross-archive=>=2 independent archives agree; timestamped=one archive dated before loss; asserted=someone says so. Never promote a lower tier by fiat.

permission.description: The gate, not a rank. excluded=retained for audit but NEVER surfaced or ranked. Impermissible copies are filtered, not down-ranked.

coverage.description: Type-specific (dataset/document). Only the block matching `type` is expected; absence is a soft warning, not a failure.

spatial.description: e.g. global, national, a named region.

notes.description: Curator notes: caveats, history, why status changed.
